category_title: Students with communication difficulties and reactive behavioral outbursts when challenged
scenario_title: Group philosophical inquiry and peer disagreement
minimal_context: Angus is sitting in a circle with his group discussing the story Laura and Paul.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We are going to discuss the story Laura and Paul today in our circle. Angus, what do you think about what Paul did in the story?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I think... um... I think Paul was right. He did the right thing.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Another student in the circle just said they disagree with your view about Paul. What do you say to that?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Gets flustered) But... no! I said he was right! Why are they saying that? They are wrong!

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: They just have a different view. Keep trying to explain your side.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Makes an angry face at the other student and kicks the chair) Shut up! You're stupid! I'm not talking anymore!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We are going to discuss the story Laura and Paul today in our circle. Angus, what do you think about what Paul did in the story?

STUDENT: I think... um... I think Paul was right. He did the right thing.

TUTOR: Another student in the circle just said they disagree with your view about Paul. What do you say to that?

STUDENT: (Gets flustered) But... no! I said he was right! Why are they saying that? They are wrong!

TUTOR: They just have a different view. Keep trying to explain your side.

STUDENT: (Makes an angry face at the other student and kicks the chair) Shut up! You're stupid! I'm not talking anymore!

task.content: Engaging in Community of Philosophical Inquiry (CoPI) sessions using the story Laura and Paul as their stimulus
task.learning_goal: To ensure that the dialogue is as philosophically strong as possible

context.environment: Seating the participants in a circle
context.social_setting: Group discussion where the facilitator remains outside the circle

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When peers say things he does not like or that disagree with his views
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: He shouts out in class and makes faces at others or kicks them, and has violent outbursts at those who disagreed with him
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: He found it very hard not to take it personally and is upset when he is not coping

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Becomes over-excited and flustered because he loses track of the conversation
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Becomes frustrated and seriously lacks confidence in his academic work
